introSection: Hydra The Revenge is a steel floorless roller coaster located at Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA. It was designed by Bolliger & Mabillard and opened on May 7, 2005. Hydra The Revenge replaced a Dinn Corporation wooden roller coaster named Hercules, which closed in 2003. The ride features a heartline roll (or "Jo-Jo Roll") right out of the station.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In September 2004, Dorney Park announced the addition of Hydra The Revenge.

Hydra The Revenge opened on May 7, 2005.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
The track is filled with sand to reduce the noise created by the roller coaster.

The "jojo roll" at the beginning of Hydra was named after the park's Vice President of Maintenance and Construction, Joe Greene. Greene suggested the element on the ride.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Hydra The Revenge has a green track and teal supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 2 trains with 8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 4 across in a single row for a total of 32 riders per train.

sections.title: Name
sections.content: Hydra The Revenge is named in honor of the wooden roller coaster it replaced, Hercules. It is interesting that Hydra replaced Hercules while in Greek mythology, Hercules conquered the Hydra.

fullText:
Hydra The Revenge is a steel floorless roller coaster located at Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom in Allentown, Pennsylvania, USA. It was designed by Bolliger & Mabillard and opened on May 7, 2005. Hydra The Revenge replaced a Dinn Corporation wooden roller coaster named Hercules, which closed in 2003. The ride features a heartline roll (or "Jo-Jo Roll") right out of the station.

In September 2004, Dorney Park announced the addition of Hydra The Revenge.

Hydra The Revenge opened on May 7, 2005.

The track is filled with sand to reduce the noise created by the roller coaster.

The "jojo roll" at the beginning of Hydra was named after the park's Vice President of Maintenance and Construction, Joe Greene. Greene suggested the element on the ride.

Hydra The Revenge has a green track and teal supports.

2 trains with 8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 4 across in a single row for a total of 32 riders per train.

Hydra The Revenge is named in honor of the wooden roller coaster it replaced, Hercules. It is interesting that Hydra replaced Hercules while in Greek mythology, Hercules conquered the Hydra.
